new_classes.definition_en: Ethyl alcohol and other spirits, denatured, of any alcoholic strength, classified under CN subheading 2207 20 (Chapter 22). Denatured alcohol has been rendered unfit for human consumption by the addition of denaturing substances.

coverage_explanation: The legal text defines CN 22072000 as ethyl alcohol and other spirits, denatured, of any strength. The key discriminating criterion — denaturation — is captured via the existing `eucn:isDenatured` boolean property (hasValue true) and the new class is anchored as a subclass of the existing `eucn:EthylAlcohol` class (heading 2207). The CN code string is fixed via `eucn:codeString`. The phrase "of any strength" implies no alcoholic-strength threshold restriction is needed, which is correctly omitted. The reference to the HS Explanatory Note fourth paragraph (concerning specific denaturing substances and methods) cannot be fully modelled without introducing highly granular substance-class axioms; this is the minor gap that prevents a score of 1.0.
